LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT-CROSS/COMPLAINANT WEBCOR BUILDERS, INC.'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE THIRD ROE AMENDMENT TO CROSS-COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. COURTS HAVE A LIBERAL POLICY OF ALLOWING AMENDMENT TO CROSS-COMPLAINTS AT "ANY STAGE OF THE PROCEEDING, UP TO AND INCLUDING TRIAL," AS LONG AS THE OTHER SIDE WILL NOT BE PREJUDICED. (ATKINSON V. ELK CORP. (2003) 109 CAL.APP.4TH 739, 761.) WEBCOR BUILDERS, INC. LEARNED ABOUT F. RODGERS INSULATION, INC., ZURN INDUSTRIES, LLC, AND WILKINS' ROLE IN THE MATTER IN SEPTEMBER 2015. ALLOWING WEBCOR BUILDERS, INC. TO FILE THE THIRD AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT WILL PROMOTE JUDICIAL ECONOMY AND PREVENT MULTIPLE SUITS. ALTHOUGH TRIAL IS SET FOR MARCH 2016, EXPERTS HAVE NOT YET BEEN DEPOSED AND THE BURDEN OF MULTIPLE SUITS IF THE MOTION IS NOT GRANTED OUTWEIGHS THE BURDEN ASSOCIATED WITH A SHORT TRIAL DELAY. THE COURT'S GRANTING OF WEBCOR BUILDERS, INC.'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A THIRD ROE AMENDMENT TO THE CROSS-COMPLAINT MEANS THAT PLAINTIFF WILL NOT BE PREJUDICED BY THE COURT'S GRANTING OF DEVELOPMENT, LLC AND BRIDGEVIEW MANAGEMENT, INC.'S MOTIONS FOR LEAVE TO FILE A FOURTH ROE AMENDMENT TO THE CROSS-COMPLAINT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER : MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489 (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Withdraw Jya Consulting Engineers, Incs Notice Of Appearance And Answers And To Dismiss Cross-Complaints
Off Calendar Dec-18-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Ericksen and Matthew Volkmann of Severson & Werson telephone number 415-398-3344 for Cross-Defendant JYA.
Norma Morales of Lorber, Greenfield & Polito, LLP telephone number 858-513-1020 appeared by telephone for Defendants Bridgeview Development, LLC and Bridgeview Management Inc.
Michael Laidlaw of Green & Hall, APC telephone number 916-367-7098 appeared by telephone for Cross-Complainant/Defendant Webcor Builders Inc.
Heather Ingle of Lee, Hernandez, Landrum & Garofalo telephone number 925-939-2000 appeared by telephone for Defendant Webcor Builders Inc.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling.
CROSS-DEFENDANT JYA CONSULTING ENGINEERS, INC.'S Motion To Withdraw JYA Consulting Engineers, Inc.'s Notice Of Appearance And Answers And To Dismiss Cross-Complaints is denied. The court denies JYA Consulting Engineer, Inc.'s motion because neither Corporations Code Sections 2010 and 2011, nor Penasquitos, Inc. v. Superior Court (1991) 53 Cal.3d 1180 state that a court must permit a dissolved corporation to withdraw from a case or that a court must dismiss complaints brought against a dissolved corporation, if the corporation no longer has undistributed assets. The language in Penasquitos, Inc. v. Superior Court indicates that it is up to the party pursuing the action to lodge a complaint against a dissolved corporation, even if the dissolved corporation no longer possesses assets. (Penasquitos, Inc. v. Superior Court (1991) 53 Cal.3d 1180, 1191-1192.) Additionally, contrary to JYA Consulting Engineer, Inc.'s position, Defendant Webcor Builders, Inc., Bridgeview Development, LLC, and Bridgeview Management, Inc.'s citation to the "Agreement Re Business Activities," which discusses the transfer of employees, contracts, leases, and property, indicates that JYA Consulting Engineer, Inc. may have a successor in interest. (Decl. Laidlaw Ex. A.) Order signed in open Court. The Court recommends continuance of the trial to allow discovery which may resolve the matter (language included in the order).

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489. (302/EG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Fourth Roe Amendment To Cross-Complaint Naming F. Rodgers Insulation, Inc. As Roe 188
Off Calendar Dec-01-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

ATTY. PAMELA BUMATAY OF THE LORBER GREENFIELD POLITO, TEL. NO. 1-858-513-1020 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT;
ATTY. MICAHEL R. LAIDLAW & ATTY. EVAN SIPPEL-FELDMAN OF GREEN & HALL, APC, TEL. NO. 1-916-367-7098 EXT. 233 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT/ CROSS-COMPLAINANT WEBCOR BUILDERS INC. BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. HEATHER N. INGLE OF LEE HERNANDEZ LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-925-939-2000 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT BRIDGEVIEW DEV. BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT BRIDGEVIEW MANAGEMENT, INC.'S Motion For Leave To File Fourth Roe Amendment To Cross-Complaint Naming F. Rodgers Insulation, Inc. As Roe 188 IS granted. Courts have a liberal policy of allowing amendment to cross-complaints at "any stage of the proceeding, up to and including trial," as long as the other side will not be prejudiced. (Atkinson v. Elk Corp. (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 739, 761.) Allowing Bridgeview Management, Inc. to file the fourth amended cross-complaint will promote judicial economy and prevent multiple suits. Although trial is set for March 2016, experts have not yet been deposed and the burden of multiple suits if the motion is not granted outweighs the burden associated with a short trial delay. The court's granting of Webcor Builders, Inc.'s motion for leave to file a third roe amendment to the cross-complaint means that Plaintiff will not be prejudiced by the court's granting of Bridgeview Management, Inc.'s motion for leave to file a fourth roe amendment to the cross-complaint.
